entry.title: Create product using OData v2
entry.content:
Hi,

Sorry, but I'm new to BTP and the Integration Suite. I'm just trying to create a Product in S/4HANA Public Edition.

When using Postman, I can create a Product using the followig payload to the endpoint https://myNNNNNN-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/A_Product:

{

    "ProductType": "FERT",

    "to_Description": [
        {
            "Product": "EDWARD01",
            "Language": "EN",
            "ProductDescription": "Edward Test 1"
        }
    ]
}

In Integration Studio, having this part:

Defined my adapter:



In my mapping:

Tried several different mapping structures. The one closed to hopefully the correct one, will be the one listed above. However, still get the error: 

I've also tried to use the downloaded edmx and then map to A_Product and A_ProductType without any success.

Can someone please point me to the correct mapping structure to be used when creating a Product?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Die SAP integrated Toolchain zur Digitalisierung, Modernisierung und End-to-End Transformation
entry.content:
In dieser spannenden Episode des EducationNewscast Podcasts spreche ich mit Florian Hofstädter über die SAP Integrated Toolchain zur Digitalisierung, Modernisierung und End-to-End-Transformation von SAP-Einführungen. Florian ist ein erfahrener SAP-Berater mit langjähriger Projekterfahrung und teilt wertvolle Einblicke in die praktische Anwendung dieser innovativen Toolchain.

Florian ist ein echtes SAP-Gewächs - vom Studium über Praktika bis hin zur Master-Thesis war er bereits eng mit SAP verbunden. Nach seinem direkten Einstieg als EWM-Berater entwickelte er sich über fünf Jahre in der Beratung weiter und wechselte dann in die Projektleitung und das Programmmanagement. Seit über drei Jahren arbeitet er in diesem Bereich und ist seit dem letzten Jahr intensiv mit der Integrated Toolchain beschäftigt, wo er sowohl die interne Awareness bei SAP als auch die Kundenbetreuung vorantreibt. Anbei eine Zusammenfassung als Blog - gern könnt ihr auch reinhören auf SAP Podcasts - Apple Podcasts oder Spotify.

Was ist die SAP Integrated Toolchain?

Die SAP Integrated Toolchain ist eine strategische Kombination aus SAP-eigenen Tools und ausgewählten Partner-Tools. Zu den SAP-Tools gehören LeanIX für Enterprise Architecture Management,  SAP Signavio für Prozessanalyse und -design, Solution Manager und Cloud-ALM für Application Lifecycle Management sowie SAP Enable Now und WalkMe für das Enablement. Ergänzt wird diese Sammlung durch Partner-Tools wie Tricentis für Testmanagement und Syniti für Datenmanagement.

Das Besondere an dieser Toolchain ist ihre Flexibilität - du kannst jederzeit damit starten, unabhängig davon, ob du RISE-Kunde bist oder nicht. Natürlich bietet RISE-Kunden Vorteile, da LinaX, Signavio und Cloud-ALM bereits Bestandteil des Pakets sind, aber auch ohne RISE kannst du sofort mit der Digitalisierung beginnen.

Toolchain Setup und Architektur

Das Setup der Toolchain ist hochgradig individuell und folgt dem pragmatischen Ansatz "es kommt darauf an". Viele Kunden haben bereits Enterprise-Architecture über LeanIX und Prozesse in Signavio implementiert - diese beiden Bausteine lassen sich hervorragend miteinander verbinden. Zusätzlich kann Cloud-ALM positioniert werden, um ein rundes Setup zu schaffen.

Der Schlüssel liegt jedoch darin, die konkreten Pain-Points der Kunden zu identifizieren. Wenn ein Kunde beispielsweise hohe Aufwände im Testmanagement hat, kann direkt Tricentis platziert werden. Steht ein Kunde am Anfang einer Transformation und möchte von alten ECC-Systemen zu S/4HANA wechseln, kommt Syniti ins Spiel.

Tool Walk-Through: Die einzelnen Komponenten

SAP-Tools

LeanIX fungiert als Enterprise Architecture Management Tool, mit dem du deine aktuelle Ist-Architektur abbilden und auch Transformationspfade planen kannst. Du siehst, wo du in sechs Monaten, zwölf Monaten oder drei Jahren stehen wirst.

Signavio ist das Herzstück für Prozessanalyse und -design. Hier kannst du Business Process Flows visualisieren, Prozesse analysieren und Optimierungen durchführen.

Cloud-ALM und Solution Manager unterstützen den gesamten Lebenszyklus von Design über Build bis zur Run-Phase. Während Cloud-ALM strategisch gesetzt ist, bleibt Solution Manager für viele Funktionalitäten relevant, die in Cloud-ALM noch nicht verfügbar sind.

SAP Enable Now und WalkMe bilden das Enablement-Duo. WalkMe wird sein Branding behalten und bietet In-System-Guidance, während Enable Now darin aufgehen wird in einem Werkzeug für digitale Lernmaterialien und Wissensmanagement.

Partner-Tools

Tricentis bringt nicht nur Testmanagement mit, sondern auch Change-Impact-Analysen, die den Aufwand für Test-Cases erheblich reduzieren kann.

Syniti spezialisiert sich auf Datenanalyse von alten ECC-Systemen und ermöglicht das Mapping von Datenobjekten auf neue SAP S/4HANA-Templates.

Praktische Beispiele und Vorteile der integrierten Toolchain

Die Integrated Toolchain bricht Silos auf und schafft echte Synergien zwischen den Tools. Ein konkretes Beispiel: Wenn du deine Architektur in LeanIX mit den Prozessen in Signavio verbindest, siehst du direkt im Prozess, welche Systeme bald aus der Wartung laufen. Oder wenn du einen Prozess in Signavio änderst, wird automatisch eine Benachrichtigung an SAP Enable Now gesendet, damit das Trainingsmaterial aktualisiert wird.

Besonders beeindruckend ist die Integration zwischen Signavio, Cloud-ALM/Solution Manager und Tricentis: Aus dem Prozessfluss können automatisiert Test-Cases angelegt werden, die bei Prozessänderungen synchronisiert angepasst werden.

Ein Praxisbeispiel verdeutlicht den Nutzen: Ein Kunde hatte einen Quartalszyklus mit 600 Testtagen ohne Testautomatisierung. Nach der Implementierung von Tricentis mit Change-Impact-Analyse und Testautomatisierung konnte dieser Aufwand drastisch reduziert werden, um einen schnelleren Innovationszyklus zu erreichen.

Rolle von KI in der Toolchain

Künstliche Intelligenz ist bereits heute in allen Tools der Toolchain integriert. SAP Enable Now bietet On-the-fly-Übersetzungen von Trainings, Signavio kann aus Fließtext Business-Process-Flows generieren, und Tricentis nutzt KI-Algorithmen für die Change-Impact-Analyse.

Diese KI-Funktionalitäten sind nur der Anfang. In Zukunft werden immer mehr AI-Features hinzukommen, die weitere Benefits liefern und wiederholende Tätigkeiten automatisieren. Das Ziel ist es, dass sich Mitarbeiter auf wichtige Analysen und Kernthemen konzentrieren können, anstatt von kleineren, wiederkehrenden Aufgaben aufgehalten zu werden.

Herausforderungen in der Praxis

Die größte Herausforderung für Kunden ist oft die Frage: "Wo fange ich an?" Digitalisierung erscheint als Riesenwort, das abschreckt. Hier hilft der pragmatische Ansatz: Identifiziere deine Pain-Points, priorisiere sie und starte mit dem wichtigsten Problem.

Die häufigsten Pain-Points sind Prozesse, Testmanagement und Daten. Zunehmend kommt auch Enterprise Architecture Management dazu - die Anzahl der Applications in mittelständischen Unternehmen ist von 200 (2020) auf 600 (2025) gestiegen.

Change Management und Training

Change Management ist ein extrem wichtiger, aber oft vernachlässigter Aspekt in Projekten. Du kannst das beste Tool zur Verfügung stellen, aber wenn Mitarbeiter die letzten zehn Jahre mit ihrem alten System gearbeitet haben, werden sie nicht von heute auf morgen das neue Tool annehmen. Der Change-Prozess muss von Anfang bis Ende begleitet werden.

WalkMe ergänzt dabei SAP Enable Now perfekt: Während Enable Now umfassende Trainings bietet, gibt WalkMe direkt im System Guidance. Der Name ist Programm - "walk me" führt dich durch Prozesse, die du nicht oft machst. Die Vision ist, dass du später kein separates Trainingssystem mehr benötigst, sondern die Guidance direkt im SAP-System und auch in Non-SAP-Systemen erhältst.

Ausblick in die Zukunft

Die Integrated Toolchain liefert bereits heute echten Mehrwert, aber mit wachsenden KI-Capabilities wird dieser Mehrwert exponentiell steigen. Wiederholende Tätigkeiten werden zunehmend automatisiert, sodass sich Teams auf strategische Analysen und Kernthemen konzentrieren können.

Die Toolchain wird sich kontinuierlich weiterentwickeln, aber der Fokus bleibt pragmatisch: Identifiziere die wichtigsten Pain-Points und gehe diese systematisch an.

Rolle von Partnern

RISE-Transformationspartner sind über die Toolchain informiert und können Transformationen bei Kunden gestalten. Dies eröffnet Partnern zusätzliche Potenziale für Projektdurchführungen und erweitert das Ökosystem der Digitalisierung.

Unpopular Opinion: Einfach anfangen!

Florians kontroverse Meinung: Kunden sind oft zu zurückhaltend bei der Digitalisierung. Heute funktioniert bereits sehr viel - man muss einfach nur damit anfangen! Anstatt lange zu diskutieren und die Transformation zu verzögern, sollten Unternehmen hands-on die Digitalisierung gemeinsam gestalten.

Wichtig dabei: Tools allein reichen nicht. Die Methodik ist genauso entscheidend. Change Management ist Teil der Methodik, und ohne strikte Governance ist auch das beste Tool zum Scheitern verurteilt. Methodik und Tools müssen Hand in Hand gehen.

Inspirierende Aussagen aus dem Podcast

  1. "Man kann jederzeit mit der Digitalisierung anfangen - es gibt keine Voraussetzungen, die einen zurückhalten sollten."

  2. "Wir wollen Silos aufbrechen und zeigen, dass die Verbindung von Tools echte Mehrwertschöpfung schafft."

  3. "Das beste Tool allein bringt nichts, wenn die Menschen nicht bereit sind, es anzunehmen - Change Management ist entscheidend."

  4. "Keep it simple, man lernt nie aus - in jedem Alter und zu jeder Zeit kann man dazulernen."

  5. "Lasst uns nicht über Digitalisierung sprechen, sondern sie hands-on gemeinsam gestalten."

Konkrete Tipps für die Praxis von Florian

  • Starte pragmatisch: Identifiziere deine größten Pain-Points und priorisiere sie

  • Denke integriert: Nutze die Synergien zwischen den Tools, anstatt sie isoliert zu betrachten

  • Investiere in Change Management: Begleite den Transformationsprozess von Anfang bis Ende

  • Nutze KI-Features: Die AI-Funktionalitäten sind bereits heute verfügbar und bringen sofortigen Nutzen

  • Baue hybride Landschaften: Du musst nicht alles auf einmal umstellen - kombiniere bestehende und neue Tools sinnvoll

Kurzes Resümee

Die SAP Integrated Toolchain ist mehr als nur eine Sammlung von Tools - sie ist ein ganzheitlicher Ansatz zur Digitalisierung von SAP-Projekten und dem Betrieb. Damit können viele Effizienzen realisiert werden, was sich schnell bezahlbar macht. Ich habe mich manchmal geefragt, wie SAP Projekte und der kontinulierliche Betrieb weiter digitalisiert werden können. Neben der Digitalsierung der eigentlichen Wertschöpfungsprozesse. Und hier ist die Toolchain eine Antwort.

Change Management und die richtige Methodik sind dabei genauso wichtig wie die technischen Capabilities. Mich freut es,  dass die so wichtigen Themen wie Lernen und Change Management dabei auch reflektiert sind. 

Es gibt übrigens auch 2 Services dazu: Den Integrated Toolchain Discovery & Advisory Service - mehr Infos in den Links unten.     

Postet Eure Fragen oder Kommentare gerne hier unten - freue mich auf eine weitere Diskussion. 

Weiterführende Links

  • Florian auf LinkedIn

  • Informationen zur Toolchain inkl. zwei Services

  • Instagram Account zum Thema Kommunikation - askvinh

  • EducationNewscast Podcast

entry.title: Re: Manage processing rules - mass creation of rules
entry.content:
Hello @Constant 

Thank you for your question. To clarify, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the app Manage Processing Rules for Bank Statements (F2919) allows:
✔Export of existing rules to Excel
✔ Modification and re-import of those rules

But creating entirely new rules via Excel upload is not supported. New rules must be created manually in the system, after which export/import can be used for adjustments.

If mass creation via Excel is essential, I suggest submitting a requirement through the SAP Customer Influence Portal.

Let me know if you need further support

Best regards,
Chuma

Sibusiso Nombembe

entry.title: Re: Programmatically switch between overview's tabs in SA...
entry.content:
I had the same requirement recently (at least what I understand to be the same requirement) and found a different solution that I like better. Rather than changing the tabs that are assigned to the viewset, I wanted to navigate directly to the right tab. 

After some debugging, I found where this is controlled: the flag 'traymode' in the overviewpage controller tells the framework which tab to load. By default, the first tab in a viewarea (rather: a tile in a vieware) is displayed (see CL_BSP_WD_OVW_VIEWSET, DO_REQUEST). This flag is found in attribute table me->view_area.

Once I found this, it was pretty easy. The method DETACH_STATIC_OVW_VIEWS (redefined in our Z-class) is called right before the DO_REQUEST method. We can simply set traymode = 'X' for the desired view and there you go!

The only thing to consider is that you can do this only for the first load of your object, because you want to be able to navigate to the other tabs manually. To do this, you can reset the flag 'first_load' to abap_false when you set traymode to abap_true and check for this value before you set the traymode flag. Somehow the first_call value remains 'X' for as long as you display your object. It is reset to 'X' when you open another object.

entry.title: Re: Instruction Key Mapping to Payment Media File
entry.content:
Hello @Michal_Pandoscak,
In the title of your post, you mention that the topic "Mapping to Payment Media File" is being addressed. You then went on to explain how and which instructions / instruction keys are created and possibly assigned to a payment method. However, I am missing the step that shows how this affects the payment media file.

For pain.001 and the instruction "14 URGP", for example, the EPC Guidelines require that the service level in the attribute <PmtInf><PmtTpInf><SvcLvl><Cd> be set to "URGP". For the instruction "25 INST", the local instrument code must be set to "INST" in the attribute <PmtInf><PmtTpInf><LclInstrm><Cd>.

I can still find corresponding coding for the SvcLvl in the method CL_IDFI_CGI_DMEE_COUNTRIES~GET_CREDIT. But for the LclInstrmCd, I can't find anything equivalent. So how is the instruction "INST" mapped into the payment media file?

Thank you,
kind regard
Thomas

entry.title: Re: Approval flow Catalog
entry.content:
Hi Fleur, 

It looks like the catalog itself is error free, but someone in the approval flow is no longer an active user. There are a few ways to get around this issue: 

  • Temporarily activate the account for the user who left the company.
  • Change the On Behalf Of user to only active users. 
  • Reject the catalog and create a new one with a new On Behalf Of user.

Hope this helps! 

-Rachel

entry.title: Re: Creating a new Crystal Report and using two Parameter...
entry.content:
OK, I'm not sur to understand your second option.

 

Option 1 : You want the records for the last month. -> Today = May 1st, 2025 to May 31, 2025.

Option 2 : You want the records for the user parameters.


If it's that, I will use 3 parameters : start date, end date and mode to use (=option #1 or #2).

 

For exemple :

 


Auto mode is the last month.

+ 2 dates parameters

 

 

Create formula field :

 

With

 

 

local datevar firstday;

local datevar lastday;

// Initiate with parameter

firstday:={?p_date1}; lastday:={?p_date2};

// If the automode, first date is the first days of current month – 1 month. Last days is the first days of current month – 1 day.


if {?p_automode} then (

  firstday:=date(DateAdd ('m', -1, cdate(year(currentdate),month(currentdate),1)));

  lastday :=date(DateAdd ('d', -1, cdate(year(currentdate),month(currentdate),1)))

);

// Then, I filter records :

{Command.dat}>=firstday and {Command.dat}<=lastday;

 

 

You put the formula in records select.

 

If you don’t want the p_automode parameter, you must check the dates parameters. Imagine date #1 is not use = initiate with old date. Date #2 is your reference date (= first date of month)

local datevar firstday;
local datevar lastday;
firstday:={?p_date1}; lastday:={?p_date2};

if firstday<date(1900,1,1) then (
  firstday:=date(DateAdd ('m', -1, cdate(year({?p_date2}),month({?p_date2}),1)));
  lastday :=date(DateAdd ('d', -1, cdate(year({?p_date2}),month({?p_date2}),1)))
);

{Command.dat}>=firstday and {Command.dat}<=lastday;

entry.title: Hide / Show dimensions on Tables and Charts in SAC in view / present mode
entry.content:
Preamble

This blog guides SAC application designers and engineers on a simple and effective solution for how to control which dimensions to be displayed on a table or chart in "view" or "present" modes. So that business user could have a flexible way to manually choose different dimensions within the same widget at run-time.

Lately I came across multiple questions in various SAP discussion boards on how to hide a particular dimension from a table, and it inspired me to start this blog to give some useful hints for achieving it.

 

Analysis

Let's take as an example a simple Sales Orders model, that gets consumed by the Sales Order table with the following three measures and four dimensions.

Model:

 

 

 

 

 

 

Table (design builder):

Table (view mode):

The goal is to have a possibility to hide any of four dimensions on this table in view mode.

There is a standard way to hide the column in a table, but only if this column is a measure.

With the new features, such as Optimized Design Experience and the ability to add script elements that consume SAC API to existing stories, it is possible to tweak standard behavior.

 

Solution approach

The idea of controlling dimension visibility in a table is to create a dropdown widget where all available dimensions would be stored and set up as shown or hidden.  Then, the business user could manually set and define a particular dimension to be shown or hidden by selecting it from the dropdown list.

The following steps instruct how to make dimensions dynamic for a table widget. For a chart, it will be very similar, with some slight adjustments that will be covered in the end of this blog.

NOTE: This solution is not hardcoded, it is neither dependent on a number of defined dimensions nor on their type (it handles hierarchies as well). It can be taken as a add-on feature to implement dynamic dimensions at run-time. 

Step1. Assuming the story structure is constructed as depicted below, add a new dropdown element (dropdownDimensionTable) into same page/lane along with the corresponding table (tableSalesOrders). No input values needed, just keep it empty from the scratch.

 

 

 

 

 

Step2. Create two new collections (as script variables) that will store dimension ID and its description values.

Set both variables as an Array.

Step3. Initialize both variables and the dropdown list with dimension ID and Name values defined in the table. The script below gets the job done. Put it into the table's page in onInitialization event.

// reset global dimensions collections
while (actualDimensionIdTable.length) { actualDimensionIdTable.pop(); }  // dimensions id
while (actualDimensionDescTable.length) { actualDimensionDescTable.pop(); }  // dimensions description

// list of all available dimension in the given table (tableSalesOrders)
var tableDimensionList = tableSalesOrders.getDimensionsOnRows();
var modelDimensionList = tableSalesOrders.getDataSource().getDimensions();

// loop through all available dimensions in chart
for (var i = 0; i < tableDimensionList.length; i++) {
	// populate global dimensions collections
	actualDimensionIdTable.push(tableDimensionList[i]);  // dimensions id
	for (var j = 0; j < modelDimensionList.length; j++) {  // dimensions description
		if (modelDimensionList[j].id === tableDimensionList[i]) {
			actualDimensionDescTable.push(modelDimensionList[j].description);
		}
	}
	// initiate dropdown items
	dropdownDimensionTable.addItem(actualDimensionDescTable[i]);
}

Step4. Create a script that implements dropdown logic by picking a particular dimension from the dropdown list:

  • Hides dimension that was visible before and add the status (HIDDEN) 
  • Shows dimension that was invisible before

The script below handles this behavior. Put it into the dropdown's onSelect event.

var pickedDimension = dropdownDimensionTable.getSelectedKey();  // picked from dropdown
var dimensionIndex = actualDimensionDescTable.indexOf(pickedDimension);  // index in the collection
var position = 0;
var idx = 0;

// Show / Hide picked Dimension in the Table
if (pickedDimension.includes(' (HIDDEN)')) {  // show
	actualDimensionDescTable[dimensionIndex] = pickedDimension.replace(' (HIDDEN)','');
	for (idx = 0; idx < dimensionIndex; idx++) {
		if (!actualDimensionDescTable[idx].includes(' (HIDDEN)')) {
			position++;
		}
	}
	tableSalesOrders.addDimensionToRows(actualDimensionIdTable[dimensionIndex], position);
} else {  // hide
	actualDimensionDescTable[dimensionIndex] = pickedDimension + ' (HIDDEN)';
	tableSalesOrders.removeDimension(actualDimensionIdTable[dimensionIndex]);
}

// alway reset dropdown list in the end, with new (changed) items 
dropdownDimensionTable.removeAllItems();
for (idx = 0; idx < actualDimensionDescTable.length; idx++) {
	dropdownDimensionTable.addItem(actualDimensionDescTable[idx]);
}

Run the Report and note the dropdown list populated with all dimensions.

 

 

 

 

Hide Sales Org Id and Partner Id by picking them from the drop down list. They got removed from the dimension columns in the table, set with status (HIDDEN) in dropdown list.

Now user can evaluate figures only for Sales Org and Delivery Status.

 

 

 

 

 

Hidden dimensions could be made visible by selecting them once again from dropdown list. Status (HIDDEN) will be unset and dimension column will be added into the table with the same position as it was defined in design builder.

Eg, make dimension Partner Id visible.

 

 

 

 

Adjustments for Charts

For charts, the solution is the same as for tables with only slight differences in steps 3 and 4: in addDimension and removeDimension use Feed as an extra parameter:

chartSalesOrders.addDimension(actualDimensionIdChart[dimensionIndex], Feed.CategoryAxis, position);
chartSalesOrders.removeDimension(actualDimensionIdChart[dimensionIndex], Feed.CategoryAxis);

 

The dashboard looks like the following:

 

 

 

 

 

Pick Partner Id dimension to hide it from the chart:

entry.title: Embedded PPDS Setup Matrix
entry.content:
Setupmatrix
The steup matrix is a configuration element used to specify the time required to switch from producing one product to another or to prepare a machine from old setup to a new production setup run.

For example Product A, B, C can be produced in same lathe machine but with different moulds. Product A and C uses same Mould 1 (1hr setup time), but product B  use different mould 2 (2hr setup time). So it means if the lathe machine has mould 1 installed we can produce product A and C in single go and if need to produce the product B we need to change the mould which requires 2 hr. 

Let us consider we need to produce the products A, B, C.

Production Sequence  1: A -> B -> C


Setup time = A (1 Hr) + B (2 Hr) + C (1 Hr) = 4 Hr

Production Sequence 2:  A -> C -> B


Setup time = A & C (1 Hr) + B (2 Hr) = 3 Hr

If we produce the product in 1st sequence we need to change the moulds for each production. So it will take 1 he extra setup time. But if we produce the Products in 2nd sequence then we take 1hr less setup time. So in the 2nd sequence the remaining 1 hr of the machine can be used for producing another product. So like this we can reduce the setup time and increase the production time.  All these can be done only if we have production resourse crunch. 

Main Part: 
Setup matrix Creation methods
1. Automatic creation
2. Manuall creation.
In automatic method characters are mandatory whereas in manuall method setup group are mandatory. Also manuall method can support campaign planning

Automatic Setupmatrix creation 1: reference SAP manufactory
For generation the setup matrix automatically, we need to define the class characters for material based on business and to create it in SAP. Consider we have 3 materials and which are produced in different forms & pack in same workcenter 'Lathe' which has 9 different moulds. 

MaterialCharacter - BulkCharacter 2 - Pack
PowerppdsPower1000ML
UltrapowerppdsUltrapower500ML
SupremeppdsSupreme750ML

Setup time required for changing moulds in lathe on product change.

 Production line A - Bulk type   Production line A - Pack type 
 PowerUltra powerSupreme  1 ltr500 ml750 ml
Power023 1 ltr012
Ultra Power202 500 ml101
Supreme430 750 ml210
 123  10.50.7

So here there will be 90 combinations of setup transistion times.
Process flow 
S1. Creation of characteristics and class - CT04, Cl02 - (and moving it to APO system using CIF See)
2. Maintain setup matrices for characteristic value /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_CHARMAT.
3. Maintain rules for the generation of setup groups using /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_GRPRULE. 4. Maintain rules for setup matrix generation with /N/SAPAPOI/CDPS_MATRULE.
5. Generate the setup matrix based on the specified rule using /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_MATGEN.
6. After generation, display the setup groups and setup matrices with /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_SETUP.
7. Creation of PP master data - MM01, CS01, CR01, CA01, C223.
8. Creation of PP order - /n/SAPAPO/RRP3
9. Running heuristics - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0
Menu Path: SAP Easy Access screen > Logistics > Advanced Planning > Master Data > Setup Group/Setup Matrix > Generate Setup Matrices.
Characteristics - CT04

Class - CL02
Maintain setup matrix - /SAPAPO/CDPS_CHARMATAfter execution click create to create new entries. Later save it and click back. Then double click on the line item. Also do it for Packtype.
Transform the setuptime table to below in excel and copy it here.Do the same for packtype characteristics 
Maintain rules for generationg setup group - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS_GRPRULE

Give the characters inside and seperator as - hypen. We can use up to 6 characters


Maintain rules for generating setup matrices - /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_MATRULEGive the values which we created before.
 Generate setup matrix - /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_MATGENDisplay setup Matrix - /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_SETUP. 
We can cross verify the values which we created. Now setup matrix is created.Master data.
Created Material Master in mm01 with class characters and dummy BOM in cs01 with no components.

Check that all characteristics moved to APO system in /N/SAPAPO/MAT1. CIF is mandatory.
Create Resource - CR01
Inside capacity header click APO resource and change the resource type to 2 - single reosurce. Setup matrix can be created only in single resource type.
In APO /N/SAPAPO/RES01 maintain the setup matrix which we created.Routing - CA01 - I am creating as single routing and assigning all the materials to it.Created production version in C223 and moved it to ppds via CURTOADV_CREATE with absolete transfer
/SAPAPO/CURTO_SIMU - Display Production Data Structures
Let us create order in /n/SAPAPO/RRP3. Inside it click edit button. Then add the available date and required quantity and save it.
See the changes in production scheduling board - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0
You can see the setup matrix time has been picked here. Even I mentioned 1 hr in routing it took the time from setup matrix.
For first order the setup time 2 hr as the switch is from material empty to Power 1000ML For 2nd order its 0 hr as the switch is happening between the power 1000ml to power 1000ml. Same can be seen is setpmatrix - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS_SETUP Let us create order for other material.
Let us schedule it and see the difference in planning board

Below the switch is between power 1000ml and ultrapower 500ml material. so the 1st setup time is 3hr and the second switch is between ultrapower and ultrapower. so the setup time is 0 hr.

Manual Setup matrix creation 2:
Manual Setup Matrix are mostly used for process industries. Consider below 3 materials are produced and are grouped under below setup groups. Transistion times are given below.

ProductSetup groups SETUPGRP1SETUPGRP2SETUPGRP3
MATLATHE01SETUPGRP1623
MATLATHE02SETUPGRP2203
MATLATHE03SETUPGRP3330

Process flow 
1. Creation of setup groups - OP43
2. Moving setupgroups to APO logical system using CFM1 and CFM2
3. Display setup groups in APO using /n/SAPAPO/CDPSC6 - Maintain Setup Groups 
4. Create the setup matrix using /n/SAPAPO/CDPSC7
5. After Creation, display the setup groups and setup matrices with /N/SAPAPO/CDPS_SETUP.
6. Creation of PP master data - MM01, CS01, CR01, CA01, C223.
7. Assign the setupgroups to recipes in C202
8. Assign the setupmatrix to Resource in /n/SAPAPO/RES01
6. Creation of PP order - /n/SAPAPO/RRP3
7. Running heuristics - /n/SAPAPO/CDPS0
Configuration:
Create Setup Group 1: OP43 or Path - SIMG - Production Planning for Process Industries - Master Data - Master Recipe - Data for Operations and Phases - Define Setup Groups and Setup Group CategoriesInside it create a setup group key


After creating setupgroups in ECC we need to create integeration model in CFM1 to create these setupgroups in APO logical system. You can add plant in general selection also in addition to setup groups. Here you can uncheck the objects if you have issue.In next popup screen click No.

Later click Generate it
Activate the integeration Model in CFM2 by clicking activate and start. Keep the CFM1, OP43, CT04 and CL02 tcodes closed to avoid data locks. At a time only one model can be activated.
After activation the setup groups will be populated to APO logical system and seen in tcode /SAPAPO/CDPSC6 - Maintain Setup Groups .
Master data:
Create Setup Matrix in - /n/SAPAPO/CDPSC7

Create setupmatrix and save it. later go back and double click it

Later give the combination manually inside and save it.
The same can be displayed in /SAPAPO/CDPS_SETUP - Display Setup Matrix 
Now create the master data like material master, BOM, Recipe, PDS. Here additional step is we need to assign the setupmatrix to recipe at operation level for each material. You can add only to setup operations in recipe.
Double click on operation No 0020 to maintain the setup group.

For MATLATHE02

Assign the setup matrix in /n/SAPAPO/RES01
Move the PDS and check it whether it has all the phase and setupgroup.Now create orders in /n/SAPAPO/RRP3. Click edit button, give date, order quantity and save it for all three materials.
Detailed scheduling Board Results
Having issue in PDS. Will update the pics later. As per above method the setup matrix to be utilized for process orders.

Assumptions in PPDS vs Reality
Previously in ECC Mrp Type XO was used to run advanced planning and the materials with XO will not be planned in MRP.
But now in HANA the MRP will plan the mateirals with MRP type XO. But MRP live parameters in MD01N will not be for ePPDS materials. Because of this ePPDS production order scheduling dates were modified by MRP and planned orders created for eppds BOM components. To overcome this remove scheduling in planned order parameters (OPU5) and use basic date scheduling in MD01N. All BOM materials above eppds material to be set active for ppds. Planning procedures to be maintained accordingly in SIMG - Advanced planning - Maintain Planning procedure. Scope Limitaion in material master to be set accordingly.

Note: /n/SAPAPO/CCR is used to solve scheduling discrepencies.

Scope Limitation in Advanced planning view of material master


Note: Blank - PPDS Entire scope

Some additional data regarding class in ECC vs APO.

Class Characters in APO are used for below.
Shelf Life Planning, Campaign Planning, Setup Matrix, Alternative Mode Resource Scheduling, Detailed Scheduling with characters, Characteristic dependent planning CDP, Variant configuration. Integeration Model should be set in CFM1 to move class characters from ECC to APO logical system
Class type in ppds vs ECC

Class TypeECCAPO
Variant Configuration300400
Batch Class023230
Material Class001001
Work center Assignment019019


No Integration Model required for data flow
Required only Organization area in use

In workcenter CR02 - More - Extra - Classification - Class type 019 can be added for Alternate mode resource schdeuling.
Configs to add class charactes in ECC and PPDS

SIMG - Logistics general - Batch management - Batch valuation - Update standard characteristics ,  to create default ppds charactersitcs
SIMG - Advanced planning - basic settings - Settings for data transfer, To move batch master to ppds

Conclusion: I hope this may help someone. Please mention if there are mistakes.

entry.title: SAP CPQ - Cart -> Product cannot be configured unless a quote is created first.
entry.content:
Dear colleagues!
We integrate SAP CPQ in another webshop. We managed to configure the sso, add custom fields, set document template, create some ui responsive templates etc. etc.

However one point currently isn't as expected. We want to enter SAP CPQ directly in a new Quote. So we forward to fsso/quotation/Cart.aspx?someParams were some presets would be done via code according to the params by code in ui responsive templates.
This leads to error message:

Product cannot be configured unless a quote is created first. Contact your administrator to enable configuring products without creating a quote first.

The presets in the carts responsive template work if I hit "new Project/Quotation" and call /quotation/Cart.aspx afterwards, however without that manual step I get the mentioned error message.

I wasn't able to find such a setting to get rid of the error. Moreover I found under General / Application parameters / Shopping Cart and Quotes a setting called Create Empty Quote First which I hoped would create an empty quote first 😄 but this doesn't solve the problem.

Any clues how to get rid of this message or make sure how an empty quote is created first?

Best regards
Marek

entry.title: Re: Actual Activity Rate Calculation without using Materi...
entry.content:
Hello @KaranChauhan 

Thank you for your patience and your concern. The previous was posted in error and was incomplete.

In your Make-to-Stock scenario without 33Q, this output is correct in S4HANA Public cloud. Variances are visible in financial reporting but do not impact inventory valuation.

Materials stay valued at Standard Price (S).

  • Activity Confirmations post using the Planned Activity Rate.
  • At period-end, the system calculates the Actual Activity Rate, compares it to the Planned Rate, and posts any Activity Price Variance to the Price Difference Account (PRD).
  • Inventory valuation remains unchanged; variances impact the P&L only.

This is standard behaviour without Actual Costing (33Q) and follows SAP's Public Cloud process.

Check the SAP Reference below

KBA 2935116 - Actual cost rate calculation logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system

 SAP Help Product Costing & Overhead Management

Hope it was helpful

Thanks and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: One-Click Export-Automating SuccessFactors EC API Metadata to Excel
entry.content:
Hi Successfolks!

In this blog, I will introduce how to use an automated Python script to mass export the SuccessFactors Employee Central OData API Data Dictionary. This aims to reduce the manual workload for SuccessFactors integration consultants when organizing integration workbooks.

Why Mass Export OData API Dictionary Matter?

Introduction:

Master data integration is a complex and time-consuming process that typically involves requirement gathering, API identification, field mapping, development, testing, and deployment. Exporting the Employee Central OData API dictionary is particularly tedious—especially when field configurations change frequently during new implementations.

Background:

There are four known ways to export the SuccessFactors OData API dictionary, but none are ideal. Most require manual steps, are inefficient, and lack user-friendliness. Therefore, a solution that is automated, efficient, real-time, and reusable is needed to accelerate the dictionary extraction process. This allows consultants to focus on strategic tasks rather than repetitive work.


Solution
Advantage
Disadvantage
Use SuccessFactors OData API Metadata Endpoint DirectlyProvides real-time API metadata; developer-friendlyLacks documentation and hard to repurpose as a delivery artifact
Use OData API Dictionary in Admin CenterTable format and support to searchRequires manual copy-pasting to Excel page-by-page; not export-friendly
Use Data Model in Integration CenterExport the table and show the table relationship via pictureNot readable easily
Use Standard API Dictionary in SAP API Business HubOffers standardized dictionary and frameworkNot environment-specific; needs updates to match customer-specific configurations

Solution

Export selected entities into a clean, formatted Excel file using the SF Metadata OData API and a Python automation script.

Key benefits of automation:

  1. Automated and Streamlined Process: Significantly reduces manual effort in exporting the OData API dictionary.
  2. Lightweight and Reusable: Can be reused across instances by adjusting a few variables in the script.
  3. Real-time responsiveness: Enables mass updates to the API dictionary instantly with one click.

How to Get Started

Prerequisite

Python environment (Refer to online tutorials for setup on Windows or Mac)

SF instance API endpoint and credentials

Process

1. Download Python Script and Other Asset via Github

Download 'EC Odata API Dictionary Extract.py' and 'SF Employee Central API AttributeV2.xlsx' into local folder via https://github.com/Berg-Song/SuccessFactors_API_Metadata_Extraction/tree/main

2. Update the variable in the script and Run

Update the variable in the script based on your instance info.

  1. API_SERVER: The API Endpoint for your instance's data center server.
  2. ENTITY_SETS: Comma-separated list of entity names to query
  3. USERNAME: The username of basic authentication
  4. PASSWORD: The password of basic authentication.

Note: OAuth2.0 version is planned for future release.

Run the script

Use an IDE (e.g., Visual Studio Code) to execute the script. An output 'EC_APIField_Metadata.xlsx' will be generated in the same directory.

EC Entity Sheet: Contains metadata for all entities listed in ENTITY_SETS

Simple EC Data API Dictionary Sheet: List the most important field attribute for the API fields. It could be customized by modifying the parameter in "simple_cols" of the script. The data is sorted by Entity, Name, Key, required attribute in order. The explanation for every attribute please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/sap-successfactors-api-reference-guide-odata-v2/odata-annotations-for-properties?locale=en-US

Final Thoughts: API Dictionary Automation Journey Starts Now

This is the initial version of the automation export tool and it may not yet be perfect. I welcome your feedback and suggestions for improvement. Future enhancements may include broader module support, improved documentation, and additional helper materials. I hope this tool helps you set up and accelerate your master data integration more efficiently.😊

entry.title: Application Frontend Service now generally available in SAP BTP Trail Account [cf us10-XXX]
entry.content:
As part of our exciting  SAP Build release, we're happy to announce the release of Application Frontend service, a new SaaS application on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Trail account for US region for hosting, serving and operating frontend applications.

SAP BTP Trial accounts can be deployed with limited to five(5) applications per subaccount.

Now, you can enjoy the following benefits of Application Frontend Service

What is Application Frontend Service?

Application Frontend is a SaaS application which is not coupled to any specific SAP BTP runtime environment or other SAP BTP applications. You can find and subscribe to the service from the SAP BTP cockpit. Application Frontend is also equipped with a service broker to support interaction flows using service instances, for example when using MTA deploy in the CF environment. 

While there is native support in the good old MTA concept for lifecycle management, Application Frontend also provides a command line interface (CLI) as a first-class tool for managing your applications. With the CLI, you can directly interact with the service and execute all actions supported by Application Frontend to enjoy fast response times and new integration opportunities.

High Level Overview of Application Frontend Service - Deployment

Prerequisites to enable Application Frontend Serivce

  • Go to your SAP BTP Trail Account - Subaccount
  • Go to Entitlements - Select Application Frontend Service
  • Select 2 checkbox for enabling the Plan (Developer and Application) 

Go to Instances and Subscription - Select Application Frontend Service from dropdown with Plan - Trial

Go to Instances and Subscription - Select Application Frontend Service with Plan - Developer

Application Frontend Service Library & CLI (Command Line)

npm install -g /appfront-cli

afctl login --sso -a <api_endpoint>

afctl push

 

Conclusion

Application Frontend Service is a game-changer for Cloud Foundry users. By simplifying UI development, enhancing scalability, and promoting consistency, AFS empowers developers to create high-quality applications more efficiently. As cloud-native development continues to evolve, integrating tools like AFS will be crucial in staying ahead of the curve and delivering exceptional user experiences.

For more detailed insights, you can check out the original blog posts : 

  • Introducing Application Frontend Service : Application Frontend Service 
  • Simple UI Applications with Application Frontend Service : DEV Community

entry.title: Re: How to Upload .mtaext File to a Node in SAP Cloud Tra...
entry.content:
Hi Mio,

If API endpoint /v2/nodes/{nodeId}/mtaExtDescriptors is not exposed via the API Hub direct use is actually not supported - However, since the the API is kind of "public" via the piper library, it makes sense to document the usage also via the API Hub.I will talk to the developers and ask them to adjust this.

As you can see in piper, the API parameters you should send are  "nodeId" (int),  file as formData, "tms-named-user" (string), "mtaVersion" (string), "description" (string). 

In addition to Project Piper, MTA descriptors (.mtaext files) can be uploaded at any time through the Cloud Transport Management UI. You will need the TransportOperator role to do this. You can upload the corresponding files via the "MTA Extension Descriptors" option of the import queue of a transport node that handles the MTA content type:

Related info: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-transport-management/sap-cloud-transport-management/upload-mta-extension-descriptors?locale=en-US

Hope this helps,

Sven

entry.title: Re: equipment in a operation item of maintenance order
entry.content:
In addition to the helpful responses already provided, I’d like to add the following perspective:

When a maintenance department adopts the ISO 14224 standard for maintenance data codification and follows a Tag number approach, a clear distinction is made between the Functional Area Location (FAL) and the Functional Equipment Location (FEL). In this context, the Tag number represents the Physical Equipment for Main Equipment & Sub-Equipment/Sub-Sub-Equipment and is treated as a Functional Location (FEL) in SAP S/4HANA.

The maintenance Work Order is typically created against this Functional Location (Tag number), not directly against the Equipment created via transaction IE01. Since Equipment can be replaced multiple times at the same Tag number, tracking maintenance history per equipment becomes essential.

To achieve this, and to allow for detailed reporting (e.g., using TCode IW49), it is important to specify the actual Equipment used in each operation within the Work Order. This enables differentiation between maintenance activities performed on different equipment units installed at the same Functional Location (Tag number) over time.

This approach ensures traceability and supports more accurate reliability and performance analysis, which aligns with ISO 14224 principles.

entry.title: Universal OData ↔ MCP Bridge; Or: How I Accidentally Made 15,000 Enterprise Services Talk to AI
entry.content:
Or: How I Accidentally Made 15,000 Enterprise Services Talk to AI

In March 2025 I realised the "hard part" of enterprise AI integration was already solved by metadata we've had for years. Two hundred MCP servers suddenly became 15,000+ – with one simple translator. 🚀

 

What Problem Are We Solving?

Hey folks! 👋 This is the logical continuation of my ABAP-native AI journey:

  • ZVDB & ZLLM brought AI into ABAP (vector search, LangChain-lite, local inference)

  • This bridge brings ABAP — and every other OData system — to AI

Enterprises sit on thousands of OData endpoints that already expose clean business semantics, yet modern AI assistants can't reach them. All that was missing was a translator.

 

The "15,000+ MCP Servers" Moment

EcosystemOData services made AI-ready
SAP (ECC → S/4 & BTP)≈ 10,000
Microsoft Dynamics≈ 1,500
Salesforce≈ 800
Oracle Cloud ERP≈ 2,000
SharePoint, ServiceNow & friends≈ 1,000

Total: 15,000+ enterprise-grade services now surface as first-class MCP tools.¹ Not toys—systems that move real money.

 

TL;DR (Four Bullets, Promise!)

  1. Code → GitHub: oisee/odata_mcp (Python) & oisee/odata_mcp_go (Go) (link in first comment)

  2. Problem → $2T of software speaks OData; 0% speaks MCP

  3. Solution → Bridge auto-discovers every entity/action; zero config

  4. Result → Your ERP landscape is AI-accessible in under two minutes

 

Why This Matters—The "So What?"

Role 
CTO / ArchitectAll legacy & cloud systems become AI-ready today—no vendor roadmap, no middleware
DeveloperOne interface for SAP, Dynamics, Oracle, Salesforce—write once, chat everywhere
Business user"Claude, approve all POs < $50k" – and it just happens
 

The "Wait, That's It?" Moment

  • OData = REST + machine-readable metadata

  • MCP = JSON-RPC + tool definitions AI assistants understand

  • Bridge = translate the metadata to tool definitions. That's it.

➡️Just translate between them—and you've unlocked the whole enterprise. 🤔

 

Two Flavours, Same Power

 PythonGo
Startup~3s~0.3s
Memory~150 MB~30 MB
Best forPrototyping & demosProduction workloads
Shared superpowersFull CRUD, function imports, CSRF juggling (hi SAP 👋), Basic auth, Cookies auth

Demo: From Skepticism to "Holy Wow"

 

 

Enterprise Reality Demo

 

The Auto-Discovery Magic ⭐

OData's metadata does 90% of the work; the bridge just speaks fluent MCP.

Want the deep dive?

Check the repo or catch me on SAP On Azure Podcast 🎙️

 

The Hidden Cost of Waiting

 Vendor AI (Joule / Copilot / Einstein)Bridge Today
AvailabilityCloud-only, phased rolloutAny OData v2/v3—even ECC 2007
CoverageSingle vendorEntire landscape
Time-to-value6-18 months< 2 minutes
Data locationVendor cloudStays on-prem / your VPC
Vendor-LockHighZero (MIT licensed)
 

Security & Governance (Because Enterprise)

  • Runs inside your network — nothing leaves unless you proxy it

  • Honours existing roles — if users can't see it in GUI or Fiori - they can't see it via MCP

  • Audit trail-ready — every request loggable with full context

No new attack surface—just a protocol translator.

 

The Ecosystem Just Shifted

When 15,000+ production services become AI-accessible overnight, MCP stops being a dev toy and becomes the enterprise standard. This isn't iteration—it's inflection.

A Day in Your New AI-Powered Life

 

09:00  "Claude, what needs my attention?"
       → 3 POs > $40k awaiting approval (SAP)
       → Customer escalation (ServiceNow)
       → Budget variance on cost center 4210 (Dynamics 365)
09:05  "Approve the POs from established vendors, escalate to Sarah, schedule variance review."
       → Done. Next?

Five minutes. One assistant. Zero UI clicks.

 

What Could Go Wrong? (Let's Be Honest)

  • Query complexity: Some OData implementations choke on deeply nested $expands

  • Rate limits: Your backend might not love 100 requests/second from an enthusiastic AI

  • Schema changes: When your OData model changes, tools need regeneration

  • Governance: "Claude, show me all salaries" works if your auth is broken

But these are solvable with basic ops hygiene—rate limiters, caching, and proper RBAC.

 

Get Rolling in Two Minutes

!bash
git clone https://github.com/oisee/odata_mcp_go.git
cd odata_mcp_go && go build
./odata-mcp --service https://your-sap/opu/odata/sap/
# watch Claude light up with brand-new tools
Total wall-clock: < 120 seconds (most of it spent finding your OData URL).

⭐Star the repo if this unlocks value for you!

 

What's Next

  • Stay tuned for next week's deep dive: "10 Mind-Blowing Things You Can Build When Your ERP Speaks AI"

But honestly? Even this changes everything.

Try It. Break It. Make It Better.

Here's what I need from you amazing people:

  1. Try it with your systems - SAP, Dynamics, whatever you've got

  2. Break it creatively - I love good bug reports

  3. Add your system's quirks - Every OData implementation is a special snowflake

  4. Share what you build - The crazier the use case, the better

Drop a PR, open an issue, or just share what happens when you connect your enterprise to AI.

Want This as an Azure Service?

Look, this open-source bridge proves the concept works. But I know what you're thinking - "Great Alice, but what about enterprise deployment, monitoring, compliance, support?"

Fair point. What if this was available as a managed Azure service? Zero setup, enterprise SLA, built-in monitoring, automatic scaling...

Drop a comment if you want that. 😉

 

P.S. Yes, it handles all the authentication properly. Yes, it respects permissions. Yes, it works with your ancient SAP system from 2007. No, I don't know why this wasn't built years ago either. (Well, actually, I might know.)

P.P.S. First prompt I'll run in production: "Claude, find every place we're leaving money on the table." 💰

 

¹ Counts derived from SAP annual report (2024), Microsoft Dynamics docs, Salesforce API catalog, Oracle ERP Cloud guides, and the public awesome-mcp-servers list; full methodology in /analysis/service-counts.csv.

#EnterpriseAI #OData #MCP #OpenSource #AIIntegrationCP #OpenSource #AIIntegration

entry.title: It is not Possible to Extend an Extension Field to the Customer Contract Header Data Source
entry.content:
Dear Support,
we have created a custom field in Sales Contract under General, so that means it should apply for the whole contract.
We would like to have this field the the data source "Contract Header". But we do not see a way to activate it in contract header, the system seems to allow only to activate in data source contract items.


Is it possible to have this field in contract header data source at all?
Thank you & best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: It is not Possible to Extend an Extension Field to th...
entry.content:
Our technical documentation states that, if a data source is not listed on the Data Sources and Reports tab from the Further Usage of the Extension Field functionality, it cannot be extended with the field. Please refer to the KBA 3567194 - Unable To Add Extension Field To Report As Data Sources Are Missing for further insights.

Therefore, this is the expected system behavior. However you might want to extend the extension field to one of the available data sources.

 

To be aware of the available Data Sources/reports for each Business Context, please kindly check the SAP Help Documentation - Data Sources Enabled for Extension Fields.

All of the information on this scenario is clearly documented on both the KBA and the Help Portal.

As an alternative, you can also submit a new Improvement Request on SAP Customer Influence website or vote for existing ones.

This way, we can kindly consider this feature to be implemented on Business ByDesign in the future, as we already did to many other customers, and improve our environment.

Please, kindly check KBA 2531827 - Functionality Currently not Available, this is the way we can provide you a better experience and keep you satisfied with our solutions.

entry.title: C4C V2 <> S4 Public Prospect to Account confirmation error
entry.content:
Hello Community,
We are working on integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA Public Cloud, using SAP Cloud Integration (CI) as the middleware.
When we convert a prospect to an account, the data is successfully replicated to S/4HANA Public Cloud — we can see the customer created in the target system. However, we are not receiving any confirmation back, and the integration fails in CI.
Upon investigation, it appears that S/4HANA Public Cloud is sending a SOAP message, whereas the pre-delivered SAP iFlow expects a JSON payload in the message mapping. This mismatch is causing the failure.

Error: com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occurred: A JSONObject text must begin with '{' at 1 [character 2 line 1].
Has anyone else encountered this issue?
Do we need to raise an SAP incident for this, or is there a known workaround or configuration that we might be missing?
Looking forward to your input. 

Best regards, Aditya

entry.title: How to provide access to only one IAM App inside a Business Role?
entry.content:
Hi, 

In SAP Public Cloud a single catalogue is configured with multiple IAM apps. Is there any way to provide access to only one app inside a Business Role?

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to provide access to only one IAM App inside a Bu...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

With IAM apps of type app authorization variant (app authorization variants) you have the flexibility to manage authorizations for business users on a more granular level by activating or deactivating individual app authorization variants according to your business requirements.

IAM apps that are bundled in a business catalog are activated by default. However, you have the option to deactivate individual IAM apps according to your business requirements. This allows you to assign only the apps that are required for a business role to it. You can follow the steps below:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Roles app.

  2. Select a business role.

  3. Go to the IAM Apps tab.

    All IAM apps that are assigned to the selected business role, for example of type app authorization variant, are displayed.

  4. If certain IAM apps are not required for the selected business role, select them and click on "Deactivate"

 

For more information, please check the following documentations:

How to Activate or Deactivate IAM Apps of Type App Authorization Variant

Work with IAM Apps of the App Authorization Variant Type

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support.
